lines: Butters and Carter are locked together at the top on 49pts like two blokes refusing to blink in a staring contest — something's got to give, and Norway showing up in both their squads means they're basically just waiting to cannibalise each other's lead.

Carter's Algeria somehow dragged a 3-3 thriller out against Austria in their final group game, which is exactly the kind of chaotic energy you want from a team you need to win a knockout tournament — good luck with that, mate.

Ellis, let's talk. Argentina are through, great, lovely, well done — but South Korea and New Zealand are already on the beach, which means you're basically a one-horse race sitting in 9th. One fuck of a horse, sure, but still.

The Foreman is propping up the table on 26pts with France and Colombia as his last two men standing after Türkiye and Curaçao — yes, Curaçao — got binned in the groups. Curaçao, mate. You drew Curaçao and somehow this is your life now.

Today's only fixture is South Africa vs Canada and nobody in this sweepstake owns either of them, so it's essentially a free game — sit back, enjoy it, and pray your teams don't bottle it when the real fixtures land tomorrow.
